sentence_reviews.text: A generative adversarial network trains a generator against a discriminator whose feedback pressures generated samples toward the data distribution.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. 2014, Generative Adversarial Nets, abstract/model description.
sentence_reviews.notes: The generator-discriminator minimax setup and pressure toward the data distribution are directly supported.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Generative adversarial networks is to connect Adversarial minimax objectives, Adversarial examples & adversarial training to downstream capabilities including Image generation, editing & design.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. 2014; Deep Learning adversarial/generative modeling background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Minimax and image-generation relevance are supported, but 'adversarial examples & adversarial training' is broader than GAN training and could be narrowed.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Image generation, editing & design
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. 2014 sample generation experiments.
sentence_reviews.notes: GANs are generative models demonstrated on images; editing/design is a downstream synthesis beyond the original paper.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. 2014 minimax game objective.
sentence_reviews.notes: The minimax objective is central to GANs.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Adversarial minimax objectives, Adversarial examples & adversarial training.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. 2014; Deep Learning optimization/generative modeling chapters.
sentence_reviews.notes: Minimax dependency is supported, but adversarial examples/training and the generic limitations are not directly evidenced as written.

sentence_reviews.text: Generative adversarial networks is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Adversarial minimax objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. 2014 minimax game equation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported by the original GAN objective.

sentence_reviews.text: Generative adversarial networks is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Adversarial examples & adversarial training.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. 2014 adversarial generator-discriminator training.
sentence_reviews.notes: GANs use adversarial training in a broad sense, but the dependency label includes adversarial examples, which are a different concept.

sentence_reviews.text: Generative adversarial networks extends or specializes the architecture family Convolutional neural networks.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. 2014 uses multilayer perceptrons; Deep Learning CNN background.
sentence_reviews.notes: GANs are not inherently specializations of CNNs, even though many image GANs use convolutional architectures.

sentence_reviews.text: Generative adversarial networks is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Goodfellow et al. 2014 neural networks; Deep Learning feedforward networks.
sentence_reviews.notes: The original GAN uses neural-network generator/discriminator components; affine/dense layers are common but not required in all GAN variants.

edge_reviews.notes: composes/common is defensible for adversarial training, but the dependency label's adversarial-example component is overbroad.
GANs may use CNNs, but they do not specialize CNNs as a model family.
composes/common is plausible for neural generator/discriminator implementations.
